why.7944: frame-wide degradation on all three axes (FWHM z +14.29, roundness z 6.07, nstars z 7.17, background normal) — the vibration/bump class; per-set culling policy (frame-wide QA cull). The only frame of 399 above any defect-side threshold on shape axes.
why.cloud_window_kept: DSC_8067-8092 + DSC_8101 (frames 127-152, 161): contiguous low-nstars window (z 3.5-4.6 low, ~200 stars below the 1857 median) with NORMAL FWHM/roundness and background up <1 z — the thin-cloud signature. KEPT: 27 of 399 frames (6.8%) is a per-pixel MINORITY everywhere (the drift moves the field ~120 px across the window, no dwelling band), and dropping a minority subset buys no matching gain and pays the sqrt(N) noise penalty (dead-end registry).
why.satellites_kept: 8 unique satellite objects across 52 frame-detections (audit_work/anomaly_audit.json), no aircraft, no unknowns — moving minority trails stack clean through in-group rejection; user-ratified 'satellites stay'.
why.session_edges_kept: DSC_7941 (first) and DSC_8339 (last) are QA-clean and not anomaly-flagged — no session-edge cull.
why.weight_null: wFWHM weighting at this set's ~4.5% FWHM spread (3.60-3.95 median 3.71) is worse than none (min-max ramp soft-culls the worst frame at any spread).
